assertions.description: Output contains exactly two complete, ready-to-post variants with only one variable differing between them, plus a labeled variable being tested
A hypothesis statement explains what it means if one variant outperforms the other, specific enough to inform future content decisions
Measurement guidance specifies a concrete metric and minimum threshold for drawing conclusions

assertion_results.note: Two complete variants isolating hook style (provocative claim vs educational framing)
Hypothesis: 'If Variant A outperforms, this audience responds more to contrarian positions than educational content'
Measurement: compare click-through rate after 48 hours or 5,000 impressions
Two variants isolating CTA type (question vs direct ask)
Hypothesis specific to freelance journalism community engagement patterns
Metric specified: comment count after 24 hours
Two variants isolating emotional register (architectural appreciation vs urban politics)
Hypothesis addresses European professional audience response to cultural vs analytical framing
Measurement: CTR after 48 hours on LinkedIn with 5,000 impression minimum

methodology: Binary assertion testing: 3 inputs (standard, minimal, European context) x 3 assertions per skill. Each assertion graded pass/fail based on whether generated output satisfies the requirement.
